Immigration should be managed to match local labor needs and infrastructure capacity.
Immigration should be managed flexibly, allowing steady inflows while matching economic capacity.

Moderate, controlled immigration that benefits host economies while preserving cultural cohesion.

—I support abortion rights in all cases.

Religion can reinforce shared values and provide social support, but it is not essential for building a good society.
Religion can help bind people through shared values and compassion, which benefits society. —But its moral impact is not enough by itself; justice, education, and health are equally essential.

(a) — religion is not needed for a good society.

Targeted cash transfers work better than blanket subsidies.
Government aid is essential—people need a safety net.

I support government support for the poor, though it should be temporary and tied to economic activity.
